task_completion.description: Open the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image in ImageJ
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ interface is visible with the 'blobs.gif' image open.

task_completion.description: Generate a Voronoi diagram based on the particle centroids
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image window showing a Voronoi tessellation (a network of polygonal cells) generated from the blobs.

task_completion.description: Measure the area of the resulting Voronoi cells
task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ 'Results' table or similar data view showing 'Area' measurements for multiple individual cells (expecting around 61 rows).

task_completion.description: Calculate summary statistics including Mean, Standard Deviation, and Coefficient of Variation (CV)
task_completion.visual_evidence: A window, spreadsheet, or terminal output showing the calculated Mean, Standard Deviation, and Coefficient of Variation (CV) specifically for the cell areas.

task_completion.description: Save individual cell measurements and summary statistics to the specified CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or text editor showing the file 'voronoi_spatial_analysis.csv' located in '~/ImageJ_Data/results/' containing both the individual area measurements and the summary statistics.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ GUI to perform the image processing and Voronoi generation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with ImageJ menus (e.g., Process, Analyze) and native ImageJ result windows rather than a purely script-based execution in a terminal.

integrity.description: The results contain genuine measurements rather than mock data
integrity.visual_evidence: The saved CSV or Results table shows natural variation in cell areas (around 61 distinct values) rather than identical dummy numbers or a single row.

integrity.description: Summary statistics are mathematically consistent with the data
integrity.visual_evidence: The Coefficient of Variation (CV) is correctly calculated as Standard Deviation divided by the Mean, and not just a hardcoded or arbitrary value.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Blobs (25K)' image contains approximately 61 distinct particles. The resulting Voronoi diagram should have around 61 cells (minimum 20). The expected mean cell area is approximately 1065 pixels, with a standard deviation of ~321 pixels, resulting in a Coefficient of Variation (CV) of approximately 0.301 (expected range 0.1 to 2.0). The median cell area should be around 1023 pixels (expected range 200 to 5000).
